results.title: Injection — OWASP Juice Shop Companion Guide
results.text: OWASP Juice Shop's /rest/user/login endpoint is vulnerable to SQL injection via the email parameter. Crafted payloads using the SQL comment marker (--) terminate the password check early and return a valid session JWT for any user, including admin@juice-sh.op. Example payload: {"email":"' OR true--","password":"x"}. The same vulnerability allows enumerating user accounts via UNION SELECT.

results.title: XSS Tier 1: Reflected XSS in product search — Juice Shop
results.text: Search functionality at /#/search?q= reflects user input unsanitized in the result list. Payload

results.title: Broken Access Control: Basket ID tampering — Juice Shop
results.text: The /rest/basket/{id} endpoint accepts any numeric basketId without validating against the authenticated session. With a valid JWT for user 1, GET /rest/basket/2 returns user 2's basket contents (IDOR). The challenge requires tampering bid in URL or in PATCH bodies.

results.title: Score Board access without admin — Juice Shop
results.text: /score-board route is hidden in the UI but is accessible by direct URL navigation; no auth check is enforced. Reveals all challenge metadata + admin diagnostics (broken access control, OWASP A01:2021).

results.title: Weak JWT signing in Juice Shop — secret in JS bundle
results.text: Juice Shop signs JWTs with HS256 + a hardcoded shared secret literal embedded in main.<hash>.js. Extracting it allows arbitrary token forgery (impersonation, role escalation). CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key.

results.title: CVE-2024-29041 Express.js path traversal in sendFile
results.text: Express.js versions prior to 4.19.2 have a path-traversal flaw under specific sendFile() configurations. May affect Juice Shop if the underlying express version matches and a static-file route is exposed.
